svg.title: Five puzzle mechanics shown side by side: match-3 grid, sliding tiles, projectile physics, pattern memory, and an AND logic gate
svg.desc: A grid of five puzzle game mechanics, each in its own card with a title above and a one-line caption below. Top row left to right. Match-3 shows a four by four grid of colored circular gems; one gem is ringed amber as the player's selection, and a horizontal row of three same-color gems is highlighted by a green pill as a match in progress. Sliding shows a four by four grid of numbered tiles one through fifteen with one empty slot on the third row; a small left-pointing arrow on the tile to the right of the empty slot indicates the next slide direction. Physics shows a small cannon at the lower left, a dashed parabolic trajectory with a red projectile drawn at its apex, and a three-ring bullseye target at the lower right where the arc lands. Bottom row left to right. Pattern shows four colored buttons arranged in a two by two grid (red, blue, green, yellow); the blue button in the top right is brightened and surrounded by a glow ring to indicate it is currently lit in the playback sequence. Logic Gates shows an AND gate as the canonical D-shaped symbol with two input wires on the left labeled A and B, and one output wire on the right labeled Q; both input wires and the output wire are colored green with green dot indicators to show all three signals are ON, with a small "= 1" annotation at the output confirming the gate evaluates to true.

g.text: Swap adjacent gems to make 3+ in a row
Slide tiles into the empty slot
Aim and launch projectiles at targets
Watch the sequence, then repeat it
Wire inputs to satisfy the output
